Q1 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A simply supported beam of width 100 mm, height 200 mm and length 2 m is made of material with Young’s modulus 200 GPa. A load of 20 kN is dropped on to the middle of the beam from a height of 40 mm. The maximum instantaneous deflection of the beam is _____ mm. (Round off to two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 4.73
Solution

4.73 (4.71 - 4.75)

Correct answer: D

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

Q4 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A thin walled cylindrical pressure vessel has inner diameter 1 m and wall thickness 20 mm is made up of a material with Young’s modulus 210 GPa and Poisson’s ratio 0.3 is subjected to internal pressure of 800 MPa. The magnitude of volumetric strain in the vessel is

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

The shear stress is

Correct answer: B
Solution

Q6 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A stepped bar as shown in the figure below is subjected to a torsional moment of 20 N-m.

If the shear modulus of the material of the shaft is 80 GPa, the angle of twist at R is _____ degrees. (Round off to two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 4.68
Solution

4.68 (4.65 - 4.71)

Q7 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A metal cube, with all faces free to deform has a Young’s modulus 210 MPa. Poisson’s ratio 0.3 and coefficient of thermal expansion 8 × 10–6 per °C. If the temperature of the cube is uniformly increased by 200°C, the volumetric strain in the cube is ________. [Correct to four decimal places]

NAT
Correct answer: 0.0048
Solution

0.0048 (0.0048 - 0.0048)

Q8 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A massless rigid beam AC of length 5 m is hinged at frictionless pin joint A and supported by a spring of stiffness 800 N/m at point B as shown in the figure.

If a load of 120 N is applied at point C, the strain energy stored in the spring is ______ N-m.
(Round off to one decimal place)

NAT
Correct answer: 25.0
Solution

25.0 (25.0 - 25.0)

The state of plane strain at a point in a two dimensional stressed element is given as

The value of principal strain at the point is/are

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, c)

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

Q14 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Two hollow cylindrical shafts A and B are made of the same material of length 1 m and wall thickness 5 mm. The outer diameter of shaft A and the inner diameter of shaft B is 40 mm. Shaft A and shaft B are subjected to twisting moments of TA and TB , respectively. If the angle of twist of both the shafts are same, the ratio of is ________. (Round off to two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 2.11
Solution

2.11 (2.08 - 2.14)

Q15 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

The principal strains in a machine component are 0.003, 0.004 and –0.002. If the modulus of elasticity and Poisson’s ratio are 120 GPa and 0.25, respectively. The magnitude of maximum principal stress is _______ MPa. (Round off to the nearest integer)

NAT
Correct answer: 624
Solution

624 (620 - 628)

Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A rectangular strain rosette is mounted on a specimen. The strain gauge p, q and r are oriented at angles 0°, 45° and 90° respectively as shown in the figure.

The strain readings in the strain gauges are obtained as
εr = –130 × 10–6; εp = 210 × 10–6 and εq = 190 × 10–6
Which of the following is/are correct?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, c)
